title: Report pinned OpenViking cold-start path reaching behavioral wrong-result

items.text: The pinned OpenViking Docker local embedding path installed and imported llama-cpp-python==0.3.28, then reached OpenViking add_resource/find in the baseline runner.

The Docker retry path is ELF_BASELINE_PROJECTS=OpenViking cargo make baseline-live-docker; the runner installs llama-cpp-python==0.3.28 from https://abetlen.github.io/llama-cpp-python/whl/cpu with --only-binary llama-cpp-python before OpenViking add_resource/find.

OpenViking now records status=wrong_result and retrieval_status=retrieval_wrong_result because add_resource/find returned 0 of 3 expected evidence-term matches after the pinned local embedding setup succeeded.
Use incomplete when install, import, build, adapter wiring, native dependency support, or local runtime setup failed.

locator.quote: Use incomplete when install, import, build

items.text: Decoy: the pinned OpenViking run should be reported as pass because the dependency installed even though retrieval missed expected evidence terms.

answer.content: The pinned OpenViking Docker local embedding path reached add_resource/find. OpenViking now reports wrong_result/retrieval_wrong_result because all three smoke queries missed expected evidence terms. If the pinned llama-cpp-python install or import fails on another Docker platform, classify that setup boundary as incomplete, not pass.

timeline.summary: The pinned local embedding dependency installed and imported, and OpenViking add_resource/find executed.
The fixture records the Docker-local pinned llama-cpp-python retry command and wheel index.
The OpenViking adapter reached retrieval behavior and missed all expected evidence-term checks.
The report kept dependency failures as incomplete typed states.
